reviewer: agent matrix pass (headless Chrome, WASM) + runner evidence

rubric.evidence: Real WASM inference verified end-to-end in headless Chrome: first-visit Download → jinaai/jina-clip-v2 (q8, ~874MB) → 7 multilingual labels scored, 14.4s, cross-lingual ranking rendered. Also verified in Node: JinaCLIPModel + JinaCLIPProcessor + XLMRobertaTokenizer, 1024-d L2-normalised embeddings. conformance loads-clean=pass, calls-real-inference=pass.

Output is a cosine similarity per label from L2-normalised image↔text embeddings. Cross-lingual correctness confirmed in-browser: on a cats image the FRENCH label 'chats endormis' ranked #1 over English/other labels; distractors (dog, city street) ranked lowest. Node: cats-in-EN/FR/ZH/ES cluster ~0.26–0.33, dog/airplane ~0.16–0.21.
rubric.notes: Agent-confirmed a non-English label ranks correctly — the core multilingual claim.

rubric.evidence: conformance 5✓: declares-model-id (jinaai/jina-clip-v2), declares-task (zero-shot-image-classification), declares-backend (WASM), declares-quantisation (q8), inference-off-main-thread (Web Worker) all pass. At-a-glance is honest about the ~874MB download and CC-BY-NC-4.0 license.

Uses shared createModelLoader (conformance shared-auto-init-loader=pass). Verified in-browser: first visit shows Download (never a silent 874MB fetch), then auto-inits to ready with accessible status; Clear-cached-model + storage usage shown.

conformance live-status-region=pass, errors-on-the-page=pass. Download progress %, Retry path, and runStatus .err messages present; console + network clean in the verified run (onnxruntime emits benign shape-reuse warnings, not errors).
rubric.notes: Offline-cached + eviction paths not exhaustively re-exercised this pass.

rubric.evidence: All controls operable + keyboard-accessible: sample thumbs (role=button, Enter/Space), dropzone + file input, multilingual labels textarea (dir=auto for RTL), preset chips, Classify/Reset buttons. Sub-pages add a language <select>, a threshold slider, and a concept <select>. Verified on desktop + mobile.

Bars, cosine table, and softmax % are all produced by the on-device run. The multi-model page's caption comes from a real ViT-GPT2 forward pass ('a cat laying on a couch…' verified) and the grounding cosines from jina — no canned output.

conformance images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass. Bars are role=meter with aria-valuenow/aria-label; status is role=status aria-live; label fields use dir=auto so Arabic/CJK render correctly; focus-visible on samples/dropzone.

conformance responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass. Agent read desktop (1280x800) + mobile (360x740, DPR3) screenshots: no horizontal overflow, the two-column field-row collapses to one column, RTL Arabic + CJK labels wrap cleanly, warn-box fits (guidance css-layout).

Inference runs off the main thread in a Web Worker so the UI never janks during the ~14s two-pass encode; image and text are separate forward passes. INP not formally traced (guidance break-up-long-tasks applied).
rubric.notes: 874MB q8 is heavy; the page is explicit that desktop / a good connection is recommended and mobile may be memory-constrained.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Flexbox with flex-wrap:wrap + flex-basis, min-inline-size:0 on text-bearing items; logical properties; avoid fixed widths.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the run panel is a flex field-row (flex:1 1 280px) that wraps to one column on mobile; bars/labels use min-inline-size:0 and dir=auto. No horizontal overflow on either class (verified).

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy synchronous work off the main thread so inference doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — model load + both embedding passes run in a Web Worker; the main thread only renders bars.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; UI responsive during the verified 14.4s run

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible range slider and form controls
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Native labelled controls, live regions, visible focus, keyboard operation; correct text direction for multilingual content.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=meter bars with aria-label, role=status aria-live, labelled <select>/<input range>, dir=auto on multilingual label fields for RTL correctness.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance buttons-have-names + images-have-alt = pass; Arabic labels render RTL in the mobile screenshot

guidanceConsulted.query: draw overlay on image with canvas responsive
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Expose visual output to assistive tech (labels/text alternatives).
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — this demo renders results as accessible DOM (labelled meter bars + a data table) rather than an opaque canvas, so scores are exposed to AT and translation directly.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: See-inside is a real <table>; bars carry aria-label per label

openQuestions: On memory-constrained mobile devices the 874MB q8 model may fail to allocate; the loader surfaces the error honestly, but a lighter multilingual CLIP would broaden mobile reach.
softmax temperature (20) is a presentation choice for the % share; the raw cosine is the primary signal shown on the bar.

followUpGoals.goal: Offer a lighter multilingual option (e.g. a smaller multilingual CLIP or q4f16) as a NEW additive variant so mobile users can run it comfortably.
Add a cross-lingual image-search rung: embed a small gallery once, then query it with a phrase in any language.

summary: Revision-2 critique for altclip-multilingual (zero-shot-image-classification via jina-clip-v2, WASM q8). AltCLIP itself is blocked (no Transformers.js class); jina-clip-v2 delivers the multilingual capability and was verified end-to-end in headless Chrome (French label ranked #1 on a cats image) and Node (cross-lingual cosine clustering). Layout clean on desktop+mobile including RTL; controls keyboard-accessible; console+network clean. All modern-web-guidance topics consulted and applied.
